Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix session persistence: explicit drain + resilient recv loop

Two bugs caused sessions to die on agent reinstall despite pty-host
keeping shells alive:

1. Health-check TCP connections triggered auto-drain in _handle_client,
   consuming ring buffer data meant for the real agent connection.
   Fix: drain is now an explicit {"cmd":"drain"} command — health
   checks (ping/pong only) never touch ring buffers.

2. recv_loop finally block put None (EOF) into ALL output queues when
   TCP connection dropped, causing ManagedSession to close sessions.
   Fix: just set _connected=False. read_blocking() now reconnects
   to pty-host (3 retries) instead of returning None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(clippy): resolve warnings across 15 crates — lifetime elision, deprecated APIs, idiomatic patterns

Systematic clippy lint fixes touching frankenterm-core, client, deps-freetype,
deps-harfbuzz, frecency, lfucache, mux-lua, open-url, tabout, and
toast-notification:

Lifetime elision (clippy::needless_lifetimes):
- Remove unused 'a lifetime parameter from MuxDomain::resolve,
  MuxPane::resolve, and MuxTab::resolve in mux-lua — the lifetime was
  declared on the &'a Arc<Mux> parameter but never used in the return type

Deprecated API replacements:
- usize::max_value() → usize::MAX in pane.rs (deprecated since Rust 1.53)
- std::io::Error::new(ErrorKind::Other, ...) → std::io::Error::other(..)
  in tabout/src/lib.rs (stabilized in Rust 1.74)

Idiomatic pattern improvements:
- match { Variant(t) => {..} _ => {} } → if let Variant(t) = .. in tab.rs
  (clippy::single_match / irrefutable_let_patterns)
- *freq = *freq / delta → *freq /= delta in lfucache (clippy::assign_op_pattern)
- Manual hasher build (build_hasher + hash + finish) → hash_one() in lfucache
  (cleaner BuildHasher API, also removes unused Hasher import)
- Octal literal 08 → 8 in frecency test (clippy::zero_prefixed_literal)
- Unnecessary &* deref-ref patterns removed in toast-notification/macos.rs

Structural fixes:
- Added LfuCache::is_empty() method (clippy::len_without_is_empty)
- Moved ?Sized bound from generic param to where clause on LfuCache::get
- Replaced LazyLock<Retained<UNUserNotificationCenter>> const with
  get_center() function in toast-notification — avoids thread-safety issues
  with the ObjC notification center singleton being captured in a static
- Simplified open-url early-return logic: the success check was unreachable
  (spawned thread, return already happened), replaced with let _ = cmd.status()

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve data loss on tab switch, ideas persistence, and multi-user conflicts

Bug 1 - Tab switch data loss:
  useClientData/useUserData hooks now use a global pendingSaves queue
  so mount-fetch waits for any in-flight unmount-save to complete.
  Empty arrays are now treated as valid data, not "no data".

Bug 2 - Ideias Infinitas losing generated ideas:
  Generated ideas were in local useState (lost on unmount).
  Now persisted via useClientData(client.id, 'generated_ideas').

Bug 3 - Multi-user client conflicts:
  After creating/deleting a client, dashboard now reloads the full
  client list from the API (source of truth) instead of relying on
  local state. Added 30s polling interval to sync between sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: route quote-to-chat to /conversations/new instead of chats list (#96)

The quote-to-chat flow was broken: clicking "Quote to chat" in the
highlight popup opened the conversations list pane instead of a new chat.

Root cause: buildQuoteRoute() returned `/conversations?attach_*` which
resolved to ConversationsPage (the list view), which ignores attach
query params entirely. No route existed for starting a new conversation
with pre-attached context.

Changes:

- Add NewConversationPage at /conversations/new that renders ChatComposer
  with conversationId={null} and reads attach_* search params to
  pre-populate context chips. On first message send, the pane navigates
  to /conversations/:id.

- Register "conversationNew" route in paneRouteRegistry with pattern
  ["conversations", "new"] placed before ["conversations", ":id"] so
  the literal "new" segment is not captured as a dynamic :id param.

- Update buildQuoteRoute() to return /conversations/new?... instead of
  /conversations?...

- Fix ChatComposer dual-router bug: both onConversationCreated callback
  and internal Next.js router.replace() were firing on new conversation
  creation. In pane context, the Next.js call would fight the pane
  router and potentially break workspace state. Now mutually exclusive:
  when onConversationCreated is provided, the parent owns navigation;
  ChatComposer only falls back to Next.js router when no callback given.

- Add useEffect sync for initialAttach in NewConversationPage to match
  ConversationPage's pattern for search param reactivity.

- Update s3_pr07_report.md to reflect the new /conversations/new route.

- Add 3 route registry tests: /conversations/new resolves correctly,
  real IDs still resolve to conversation, query params preserved.

Verified: tsc --noEmit clean, 372/372 tests pass, next build succeeds.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(slice-5): resolve 3 bugs found during QA

1. Event-loop bug in Celery worker: Redis singleton bound to a closed loop
   causing cache_delete_pattern to throw "Event loop is closed" and mark
   ingestion runs as error even when all documents processed successfully.
   Fix: reset _redis singleton before execute_run and close it after; wrap
   cache invalidation in try/except so it never fails a run.

2. Stale runs cache: finished runs not visible until 30s TTL expired.
   Fix: invalidate ingestion:runs:* cache after every run finishes.

3. Test patch target wrong: patched api.services.ingestion.run_ingestion_task
   but the import is lazy (inside trigger_run), so module attr didn't exist.
   Fix: patch api.workers.tasks.run_ingestion_task instead.
   Also: _cleanup now invalidates Redis cache keys to prevent stale-cache
   test failures on sources list assertion.

commit.message:
feat: make accessible_paths optional during agent creation (#13126)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
Users were **required** to manually select at least one workspace folder
via the OS file picker when creating an agent. The creation form would
block submission if no `accessible_paths` were provided.

After this PR:
The `accessible_paths` field is **optional** during agent creation. When
no paths are provided, the backend automatically creates a default
workspace at `{userData}/Data/agents/{agentId}/`. Users can still
optionally add paths during creation or change them later in Agent
Settings.

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The mandatory folder picker adds friction to agent creation — especially
for new users who may not know what folder to pick. The backend already
had fallback logic to create a default workspace when `accessible_paths`
is empty, so this change simply removes the frontend enforcement that
prevented that path from being reached.

The following tradeoffs were made:
- The Zod schema `accessible_paths` constraint was relaxed from
`.nonempty()` to `.array(z.string())`. This is safe because the backend
handles the empty case.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Having the frontend auto-fill a placeholder path before submission —
rejected because the backend already handles this cleanly.

### Breaking changes

None. The backend behavior is unchanged — it already supported empty
`accessible_paths` and auto-created a default workspace.

### Special notes for your reviewer

- The backend `AgentService.createAgent()` (lines 40-43) already had the
default workspace logic. No backend changes were needed.
- In `AccessibleDirsSetting.tsx`, the restriction preventing removal of
the last path was also removed, since the backend always ensures a valid
workspace exists.

### Checklist

- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future
contributors
- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can
understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans)
and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy
Scout
Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [ ] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and
addressed if required
- [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com)
was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only
when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior.
- [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via
[`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`,
or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others

### Release note

```release-note
Agent creation no longer requires selecting a workspace folder. A default workspace is automatically created under the app's data directory. Users can change or add workspace paths later in Agent Settings.
```

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

user's summaries (including drafts). Updated the get_public_summaries
RPC to support p_sort='mine' which filters by user_id instead of
requiring is_public+published. Draft summaries show an amber badge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix restore leaving lights off: race condition + Off cascade

Two bugs:

1. Race condition (cycle effects): the police/cycle effect's background
   thread could have a preview_effect API call in-flight when _restore()
   fires. That call arrived at the Trimlight cloud after SWITCH_TIMER,
   reverting the device back to Manual mode. The device then switched
   itself to Off. Fix: sleep 1.5s after state→RESTORING to let any
   in-flight API call complete before sending restore commands.

2. Off cascade: once any alarm triggered while switchState=0 (Off), every
   subsequent alarm saved 0 and restored to Off — permanently locking the
   lights off. switchState=0 when an alarm fires means the schedule was
   not running at that moment (e.g. lights off between schedule windows),
   not that the user manually turned lights off. Fix: treat SWITCH_OFF the
   same as SWITCH_TIMER on restore — always return to Timer mode so the
   schedule can resume. Only Manual mode with a specific saved effect ID
   restores to Manual.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
